page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processors on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 12 physical cores and single-thread-per-core execution. The system features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 10.67 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, but comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The server is equipped with two NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs, providing a combined total of 96 GB of VRAM. This hardware configuration is designed for workloads requiring parallel processing capabilities, such as machine learning, rendering, and large language model execution.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-core CPU tasks, including stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Large-block memory write bandwidth also ranks in the top tier, while large-block read bandwidth is strong. Multi-core CPU performance, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range. For LLM inference, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small models and strong performance with medium-sized models.

Given the inclusion of dual enterprise-grade GPUs and a high memory-to-core ratio, this server offers a specialized hardware profile. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is suited for GPU-accelerated workloads, including small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, and parallel computing tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a KVM-based virtual server designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It features 12 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. The primary hardware highlight is the inclusion of two NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs, delivering a total of 96 GB of VRAM. While the server lacks local storage, it provides strong memory bandwidth and top-tier single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by Geekbench and stress-ng benchmarks. Multi-core performance and database operations fall in the average tier. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning, small-to-medium large language model inference, image processing, and compute-intensive tasks requiring significant parallel processing capabilities.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a KVM virtual server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, optimized for parallel computing and LLM inference.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a KVM server with 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs for machine learning workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU virtual server featuring ninety-six gigabytes of VRAM and high-speed AMD EPYC processors for parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM
12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processors
128.0 GB of system memory with 10.67 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and large-block memory write performance
No local storage included, requiring external storage attachments
Best suited for LLM inference, image processing, and parallel computing
